footer.tagline: Europe's last wild river. An untamed stretch of Vjosa waiting for you in Përmet, Albania.

faq.title: Common questions
faq.whatsappReply: We reply on WhatsApp within the hour, April through October.

whyVjosa.title: Why the Vjosa is worth protecting
whyVjosa.subtitle: Every booking funds a river that almost wasn't here.

guides.title: Your guides
guides.subtitle: Every trip is led by a certified guide who knows the Përmet canyon personally.

erald.bio: Grew up in Përmet, twenty minutes from the put-in. Erald started as a safety kayaker at 19 and has run this canyon more times than he can count. He picks the day's route by reading the morning water, not a schedule.
erald.specialty: Reading water levels and live route decisions
erald.fact: Can name all 14 rapids on the Përmet stretch without looking at the river.

luka.bio: Swiftwater rescue certified and the unofficial camp DJ. Luka came to rafting from competitive swimming — he never really left the water. First-timers always get Luka. He has a gift for turning nerves into laughter before the first drop.
luka.fact: Plays guitar at every overnight camp. Bring requests.

megi.bio: Former Albanian national kayak team. Megi now designs every safety briefing guests receive before launch. She has paddled the full 272km of the Vjosa solo — in six days — to map the water personally.
megi.specialty: Swiftwater rescue protocol and risk assessment
megi.fact: Paddled the entire Vjosa solo, source to sea, in 6 days.

klausjo.bio: Shoots from a support kayak while you run the rapids. Studied photography in Tirana before moving to Përmet full-time. His same-day gallery means you leave with the entire trip in your camera roll.
klausjo.specialty: Action photography and same-day editing from the water
klausjo.fact: Personal archive of over 40,000 Vjosa river photos.

sara.bio: Teaches first-timers to roll a kayak in a single afternoon session. Sara also coaches the Përmet youth paddling club on weekends — a project she started herself in 2020.
sara.specialty: Inflatable kayak coaching for all levels
sara.fact: Taught her grandmother to kayak at age 68.

about.title: The last free-flowing river in Europe
about.paragraph1: The Vjosa runs 272km from the Pindus Mountains in Greece to the Adriatic — without a single dam, weir, or diversion along the way. It was declared Europe's first Wild River National Park in 2023, protecting the braided channels, gravel bars, and limestone canyons that most European rivers lost a century ago.
about.paragraph2: We've been guiding the Përmet canyon section since 2014 — before the park existed. Every route is chosen for the same reason the river itself was protected: it's simply too good to alter.

riverFacts.length: Total length, source to sea — entirely free-flowing
riverFacts.tributaries: Tributaries feeding the main channel along its course
riverFacts.year: Year the Vjosa became a National Park
riverFacts.countries: Countries the watershed touches: Greece, Albania, the Ionian Sea

riverProfile.stop0.name: Pindus Mountains, Greece
riverProfile.stop0.desc: The Aoös is born from snowmelt high in the Pindus range.
riverProfile.stop80.name: Crosses into Albania
riverProfile.stop80.desc: Becomes the Vjosa, widening through the Përmet valley.
riverProfile.stop145.name: Përmet — our base
riverProfile.stop145.desc: Canyon walls, gravel bars, and the rapids we run.
riverProfile.stop272.name: Adriatic Sea
riverProfile.stop272.desc: Empties into the sea near Narta Lagoon, untouched by a single dam.

offerings.title: Six ways down the river
offerings.subtitle: From a first paddle stroke to a two-day wild camp — every trip runs on the same stretch of protected canyon, just at a different pace.

halfDay.title: Half-Day Descent
halfDay.desc: A 2.5-hour run through the Class II–III canyon stretch. The classic first-timer route.

fullDay.title: Full-Day Expedition
fullDay.desc: The complete Përmet canyon, two swim stops, riverside lunch, and the longest stretch of whitewater we run.

kayak.title: Kayak Coaching
kayak.desc: One-on-one instruction in inflatable kayaks for people who want to paddle their own line.

family.title: Family Float
family.desc: A gentler, slower-moving route built for kids 8+ and first-time paddlers of any age.

photo.title: Photo & Film Trips
photo.desc: A guide shoots from a support raft and hands you a same-day gallery, no extra charge.

multiDay.title: Multi-Day Camps
multiDay.desc: Two-day descents with a wild camp on a gravel beach under the canyon walls.

meet.title: Meet & Gear Up
meet.desc: Check in at the Vjosa Rafting Center in Përmet. Get fitted with wetsuit, helmet, life jacket, and paddle. Arrive 10–15 min early.

transfer.title: Transfer & Briefing
transfer.desc: We drive you to the river entry point. Guides walk you through paddle technique, water safety, and how to read the river.

warmup.title: Warm-Up Float
warmup.desc: Start on a calm section — feel the current, find your rhythm, and take in the canyon walls. First swim stop if conditions allow.

rapids.title: Rapids & Rock Jumps
rapids.desc: Navigate Class II–III rapids through Europe's last wild river. Optional rock jumps at the swimming break.

return.title: Back to Përmet
return.desc: Vans meet you at the take-out and bring you back to the center. Free photos from your trip are ready to download the same day.

sustainability.title: We protect what we paddle
sustainability.text: The Vjosa is one of Europe's last intact river ecosystems. We operate exclusively within the bounds of the National Park permit system, carry out zero-waste trips, and actively support EcoAlbania's conservation work. No plastic single-use items. No noise pollution. No shortcuts.

items.plastic: Zero single-use plastic on every trip
items.wildlife: Fish and wildlife disturbance avoidance zones

finalCta.eyebrow: Trips fill fast in July & August
finalCta.title: Ready to get on the water?
finalCta.subtitle: Lock in your date now — or reach out on WhatsApp if you have questions first.

faq.experience.a: None at all. Our tours are designed for complete beginners. A full safety briefing is included before every departure.
faq.bring.a: Swimsuit, towel, and sunscreen. We provide wetsuits, helmets, life jackets, paddles, and dry bags.
faq.getThere.q: How do I get to Përmet?
faq.getThere.a: ~3 hours by car from Tirana, or by daily furgon from Qafa e Botës station. We can advise on transfers.
faq.group.q: Can we book as a group?
faq.group.a: Yes — groups of 6+ get a dedicated guide and preferential pricing. Contact us before booking online.
faq.cancellation.a: Full refund up to 24 hours before departure. You can reschedule once for free within 24 hours.
faq.children.a: Yes. Classic tours are suitable for children 8+. Guides adjust the route based on your group's comfort level.

header.title: Contact us
header.subtitle: Group bookings, custom trips, or just a question about the river — we're based in Përmet and respond quickly during season.

faq1.a: No. Our easy and moderate tours are suitable for complete beginners.

faq2.a: A swimsuit, sunscreen, and a change of clothes. We provide everything else.

faq3.a: Yes — for 8 or more people, contact us for custom pricing.

faq4.q: What's the best time to visit?
faq4.a: June–September for warm water and clear days. April and October are quieter and equally beautiful.

sidebar.note: 🌿 We're a small local team based in Përmet. Every message goes directly to the guides — not a call center.

hero.title:
Why the Vjosa
is worth protecting
hero.subtitle: Every booking funds a river that almost wasn't here. This is the ecology, the fight, and the National Park status behind the water you'll paddle.

fish.title: 40+ fish species
fish.desc: Including the endemic Vjosa nase, found nowhere else on Earth.

species.title: 1,100+ recorded species
species.desc: Across the Vjosa watershed: birds, mammals, amphibians, and aquatic insects, several found only here.

morphology.title: Braided river morphology
morphology.desc: One of the last European rivers still free to shift its own channels, gravel bars, and islands naturally.

dams.title: 270km, zero dams
dams.desc: A continuous, unbroken corridor from the Pindus Mountains to the Adriatic Sea.

timeline.title: From dam plans to national park

fight.title: The fight begins
fight.desc: Plans for a string of hydropower dams on the Vjosa spark a local and international campaign to protect the river.

patagonia.title: Patagonia steps in
patagonia.desc: The 'Blue Heart of Europe' campaign, backed by Patagonia and EcoAlbania, brings global attention to the Vjosa.

shelved.title: Dam plans shelved
shelved.desc: The Albanian government commits to protecting the river instead of damming it.

park.title: National Park declared
park.desc: The Vjosa becomes Europe's first Wild River National Park, protecting 12,727 hectares along its length.

today.title: Low-impact tourism
today.desc: Licensed operators like us run trips under park rules designed to fund conservation without degrading the river.

commitment.title: Tourism that funds, not threatens
commitment.paragraph1: We operate under the Vjosa Wild River National Park permit system, which caps the number of operators and trips allowed on the water. A share of every booking supports EcoAlbania's ongoing river monitoring and advocacy work.
commitment.paragraph2: No motorised boats. No permanent structures on the riverbank. No group left without a leave-no-trace briefing before launch.
